notes: The doctrinally load-bearing terms are avyaktādīni / vyakta-madhyāni / avyakta-nidhanāni (the three-phase compound) and paridevanā (lamentation). The engineering layer preserves the three-phase shape exactly: pre-existence as need (avyakta-ādi), manifest implementation (vyakta-madhya), post-existence as residual specification (avyakta-nidhana). The rhetorical question 'tatra kā paridevanā' is preserved as 'What lamentation is there in this?' The rendering follows Shankara's reading (composite beings) and Ramanuja's epistemic reframing (previous and future stages unknown) without conflating them — both readings reach the same conclusion that the visible middle is the smallest of the three. PASS.
